theme.rationale: The provided logo is a black wordmark, necessitating a light or high-contrast theme to ensure visibility and premium feel. We will use an Awwwards-style Brutalist/Luxury light theme with Tech Cobalt Blue accents.

layout_and_grids.spacing_philosophy: Generous Spacing (p-12 to p-32). Extreme negative space to elevate the premium feel.

visual_enhancers.shadows: shadow-[0_8px_32px_rgba(0,71,255,0.08)] for primary buttons and elevated cards
visual_enhancers.image_treatment: Clipped frames (rounded-2xl or arched), spotlight effects using subtle radial gradients.

motion.hero_reveal: Masked line-by-line reveal. Text inside overflow-hidden spans, translating y from 100% to 0 over 0.8s with cubic-bezier ease.
motion.scroll_reveals: Sections fade in and translate up slightly on scroll using Framer Motion (whileInView).
motion.parallax: Subtle y-axis translation on the hero iPhone mockup using Framer Motion useScroll.
motion.marquee: react-fast-marquee for slow, continuous editorial typography ribbon.

accessibility.contrast: APCA compliant. White text on Cobalt Blue has high contrast.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements (buttons, inputs, accordions) MUST include data-testid attribute in kebab-case.

components: Primary CTA Button (Pill shaped, Cobalt Blue, 'App kostenlos testen')

instructions_to_main_agent: INSTALL REQUIRED PACKAGES: Run `yarn add framer-motion @studio-freight/lenis react-fast-marquee @phosphor-icons/react`.
FONTS: Import `Outfit` (weights 300, 400, 600, 800) and `Manrope` (weights 400, 500, 600) from Google Fonts in index.css or via next/font if applicable. Apply to the CSS variables.
HERO REVEAL: Implement the signature on-load moment using Framer Motion. Split the H1 into lines, wrap each in an overflow-hidden span, and animate a child motion.span translating Y from 100% to 0%.
SMOOTH SCROLLING: Initialize Lenis at the root of the app to provide momentum scrolling. This is critical for the premium Awwwards feel.

IPHONE MOCKUP: Do not just use a static image of an iPhone. Build a CSS frame (rounded-[3rem], border-8 border-black) or use an SVG frame, and place the voice-app UI and background image inside it. Apply a useScroll parallax effect to this entire container.
DATA-TESTID: Ensure EVERY button, input, and interactive element has a data-testid property (e.g., data-testid='hero-cta-button', data-testid='waitlist-input').
COMPONENTS: Use Shadcn UI for Input, Button, and Accordion. Customize them heavily to remove default Vercel aesthetics—use the Cobalt Blue accent and generous padding.
Ensure all `.js`/`.jsx` files are used if this is a standard React setup, avoiding `.tsx` if Typescript is not configured.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Use asymmetrical grids (Bento style) and extreme negative space.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., transition: all). Always add transitions for specific properties.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, i.e. do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons. Use Phosphor Icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Missing categories (especially logos) = you must use the provided image URLs. NEVER use placeholder text.
